title: erodium jacquinianum subsp. sabulicola
intro: Erodium jacquinianum subsp. sabulicola, also known as the sand-cross or coastal herb, is a subspecies of the Erodium jacquinianum plant, which belongs to the geranium family. It is a low-growing, annual or biennial herb with small pink flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions, as well as having potential health benefits in reducing fever and pain. The herbal preparations of Erodium jacquinianum subsp. sabulicola include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
